title: For Macron, the legalization of aid in dying meant a long personal journey
subhead: After doubting the need for new legislation, fearing he might upset religious leaders and concerned about dividing French society, the president eventually resolved to support the legislation, under pressure from MPs and public opinion.

subhead: Environment Minister Monique Barbut said the drought was 'exceptionally' early France, which is sweltering under its third heatwave of the summer.

title: US targets ICC in new offensive
subhead: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio reopened a diplomatic campaign on Monday aimed at dismantling the ICC by combining sanctions, putting pressure on member states and seeking allies among major powers hostile to the institution.

title: Why France's national weather service struggles to accurately predict heatwaves
subhead: Days ahead of the heatwaves of June and July, Météo-France slightly underestimated their intensity and duration. The reason has to do with limits in forecasting models for extreme events, as well as the challenge of explaining the uncertainty of weather predictions to the general public.

title: 2026 World Cup: How France's American dream built up speed – and crashed
subhead: France fell hard to Spain on Tuesday in the semifinals of the football tournament, marking a cruel and unexpected ending for Didier Deschamps' squad, who had delighted supporters since the start of the competition.

How the 2016 Nice terrorist attack unfolded, minute-by-minute
subhead: On July 14, 2016, a terrorist drove a truck into crowds gathered on the famed Promenade des Anglais in the southern French city, killing 86 people. Ten years later, Le Monde looks back at how the tragedy unfolded.

title: Macron and Zelensky, a special relationship marked by misunderstandings
subhead: Ukrainian soldiers will join France's annual Bastille Day military parade in Paris on Tuesday, July 14. French President Emmanuel Macron and his Ukrainian counterpart Volodymyr Zelensky will stand together in a sign of Paris's support for Kyiv, but their relationship has not always been so straightforward.

title: 'You're expected to perform, but no one wants to know how you do it': Cocaine, the toxic fuel of young professionals
subhead: Cocaine at work (1/3). Among young professionals, the illegal and highly addictive drug is used in a relentless pursuit of performance, often shrouded in taboo and without questioning of working conditions.
